texto: What could he have against love? Sure, it sucked sometimes, but what was the point of living if there wasn't love?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Chani Lynn Feener que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “What could he have against love? Sure, it sucked sometimes, but what was the point of living if t…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre vida, hell, love, com foco em vida e love.

texto: We all know how it says it’s going to end, Dreamer, but no one knows for sure. That’s up to you, and your friends. It is the Four who shall decide whether or not it comes true, not a bunch of words written on a piece of paper. Not even,” he turned the book around so that its insides were facing her, “if it’s written in on very old, very large, dusty paper.
explicacao: Citação de Chani Lynn Feener sobre temas ligados a motivacao. “We all know how it says it’s going to end, Dreamer, but no one knows for sure. That’s up to you, …” articula motivacao, fantasy, reflexao em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.
